name: Shrimp & Barbecued Pork Fried Rice
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Cooked long-grain rice, preferably cold
1/2 cup Cooked shrimp, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
1/2 cup Chinese barbecue pork, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
1/2 cup Leftover cooked chicken, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
1/2 cup Fresh or frozen peas, blanched
recipeInstructions:
1. Gently rub clumps of rice between palms to break them up and transfer to a large bowl.
2. Heat wok over medium-high heat until hot. Add oil and tilt wok to coat sides.
3. When oil is moderately hot, add salt and optional shrimp paste; stir for 5 seconds.
4. Add rice and quickly stir-fry, pressing and poking at clumps until all grains are separated (about 3 minutes).
5. Season with sugar, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. Stir-fry until each grain is evenly coated (about 1 minute).
6. Push rice to the sides of the wok. Add beaten egg mixture to the center and cook for about 1 minute, lightly beating eggs only in the center.
7. Toss together with rice, incorporating small flecks of egg into the rice.
8. Add shrimp, barbecue pork, chicken, peas, lettuce, and green onions; stir until mixed and heated through and lettuce is wilted (about 2 minutes).
